message:
fix: add context timeouts to all external commands, harden path handling and resource cleanup

Context timeouts (5-10s):
- clipboard: introduce clipCmd/clipCmdRun/clipCmdOutput helpers with 5s
  context timeout, replace all raw exec.Command calls across pbcopy,
  wl-copy, xclip, xsel, WSL, and tmux clipboard backends
- git/root.go: 5s timeout on git rev-parse --show-toplevel
- git/service.go: add sync.Mutex to WorktreeService for concurrent map
  access safety; shell-quote worktree paths in cd commands
- git/worktree.go: add safeSessionPrefix() to prevent slice-bounds panic
  on short session IDs; add 5s context timeouts to git commands
- handoff/generator.go: 5-10s context timeouts on all git and beads
  subprocesses
- summary/generator.go: single 10s context shared across git diff,
  ls-files, and diff --diff-filter=D commands

Path sanitization:
- cli/extract.go: use filepath.Clean(block.FilePath) consistently for
  display, existence checks, directory creation, and writes — prevents
  path traversal via double-dot segments. Create parent directories
  automatically for nested extracted paths.

Resource cleanup:
- cli/activity.go: add done channel to signal goroutine so it does not
  leak when runActivityWatch returns normally (not via signal)
- handoff/reader.go: guard truncateForLog against maxLen <= 3

Performance:
- cass/inject.go: compile code-block and code-snippet regexes once at
  package level instead of per-call; move stopWords map to package level

Testing:
- watcher/file_reservation_test.go: 4 new tests for pane output
  deduplication, reservation refresh on output change, idle release
  output-state retention, and initial output seeding

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -209,43 +209,49 @@ func tokenize(text string) []string {
 	return words
 }
 
+// Compiled once at package level to avoid repeated compilation in hot paths.
+var (
+	codeBlockFenceRe = regexp.MustCompile("(?s)```.*?```")
+	codeBlockInlineRe = regexp.MustCompile("`[^`]+`")
+)
+
 func removeCodeBlocks(text string) string {
-	re := regexp.MustCompile("(?s)```.*?```")
-	text = re.ReplaceAllString(text, " ")
-	re = regexp.MustCompile("`[^`]+`")
-	text = re.ReplaceAllString(text, " ")
+	text = codeBlockFenceRe.ReplaceAllString(text, " ")
+	text = codeBlockInlineRe.ReplaceAllString(text, " ")
 	return text
 }
 
+// stopWords is allocated once at package level to avoid per-call map creation.
+var stopWords = map[string]bool{
+	"the": true, "a": true, "an": true, "and": true, "or": true,

+	"when": true, "where": true, "which": true, "who": true, "why": true,
+	"new": true, "use": true, "used": true, "using": true,
+	"make": true, "made": true, "like": true, "want": true, "need": true,
+	"please": true, "help": true, "here": true, "there": true,
+	"code": true, "file": true, "function": true, "method": true,
+	"class": true, "variable": true, "add": true, "create": true,
+	"update": true, "delete": true, "remove": true, "change": true,
+	"fix": true, "bug": true, "error": true, "test": true, "write": true,
+	"read": true, "run": true, "start": true, "stop": true,
+}
+

@@ -491,8 +497,11 @@ func cleanContentForMarkdown(content string) string {

+// codeSnippetRe compiled once at package level.
+var codeSnippetRe = regexp.MustCompile("(?s)```[a-z]*\n(.*?)```")
+
 func extractCodeSnippets(content string) string {
-	codePattern := regexp.MustCompile("(?s)```[a-z]*\n(.*?)```")
+	codePattern := codeSnippetRe
 	matches := codePattern.FindAllStringSubmatch(content, -1)
 	var snippets []string
 	for _, match := range matches {
